name: Seed 2.1 Pro
archDisplay: Proprietary deep-thinking agent model, architecture undisclosed
note: ByteDance's newest flagship (successor to Seed 2.0 Pro from Feb 2026), positioned by ByteDance as competitive with Claude Opus 4.6 at far lower cost; not in corpus (corpus's newest ByteDance entry is Doubao-Seed-1.6 from 2025-06-11). Pricing converted from RMB (6/30 per million input/output) at ~7.1 RMB/USD.

results.notes: Seed2.1 Pro column of ByteDance's official launch-page benchmark table
Labeled 'Terminal Bench 2.1' by ByteDance; part of the Terminal-Bench 2.x lineage (also tracked as 'Terminal-Bench v2.1' by Artificial Analysis), the harder successor to Terminal-Bench 1.0
Seed2.1 Pro column of ByteDance's official launch-page benchmark table

MathVision, no-tool configuration, from ByteDance's official Seed 2.1 launch page benchmark table (Multimodal & Vision section). With tool use, Seed 2.1 Pro scored 94.5% per the same table (Seed 2.1 Turbo: 90.1% no-tool / 92.7% with tool, for comparison).
MMMU-Pro, no-tool configuration, same official table. With tool use scored 82.7% (Turbo: 80.1% / 82.2%).

Labeled 'CharXiv-RQ' (reasoning-questions subset) on the official table, no-tool configuration -- matches this corpus's established convention of mapping the RQ/reasoning subset specifically to the 'charxiv' slug (see e.g. the kimi-k2-5, kimi-k2-6, and exaone-4-5 entries in data/results/charxiv.csv, which use the identical convention). With tool use scored 86.4% (Turbo: 82.5% / 83.6%).

GDPval win/tie rate, Table 1 ('General-agent automatic evaluation') of ByteDance's official Seed2.1 Model Card PDF (linked directly from the official seed.bytedance.com/en/seed2_1 launch page as 'Model Card'). Seed2.1-Pro scores 87.9, the best listed result, ahead of GPT-5.5 (84.9), Claude-4.7-Opus (82.7) and Gemini-3.1-Pro (67.3); confirmed in the accompanying prose ('On GDPval, Seed2.1-Pro achieves the best listed score at 87.9...'). Cross-validated: the GPT-5.5=84.9 figure in this same row exactly matches OpenAI's own self-reported GDPval score for GPT-5.5 already in this corpus's gdpval.csv, confirming both vendors are reporting the identical win-or-tie-rate metric on the same 0-100 scale (i.e. the original OpenAI GDPval benchmark, not the Elo-scaled 'GDPval-AA' Artificial Analysis variant).

Agents' Last Exam (ALE), Table 1 of the official Seed2.1 Model Card PDF. ByteDance reports Seed2.1-Pro as Pass@1/Score = 19.5/41.4; I use the Score component (41.4) as the headline figure, since it is independently corroborated by llm-stats.com's public Agents' Last Exam leaderboard, which lists Seed 2.1 Pro's score as 41.4% (0.414) with no other models yet tracked. Confirmed in the model card's prose ('On Agents' Last Exam, Seed2.1-Pro reaches 19.5 / 41.4, outperforming Claude-4.7-Opus and Gemini-3.1-Pro while approaching GPT-5.5').

BrowseComp (with Search -- the benchmark's standard/only mode, since it is unsolvable without browsing access), Table 11 ('Fundamental language capability evaluation') of the official model card, citing the original OpenAI BrowseComp paper (Wei et al.) as its reference. Seed2.1-Pro scores 86.2, the highest of the five compared (GPT-5.5 84.4, Claude-4.7-Opus 79.3, Gemini-3.1-Pro 85.9, Seed2.1-Turbo 84.9).

HLE-Verified (no tool), Table 11 of the official model card, citing the original Humanity's Last Exam paper (Phan et al., arXiv:2501.14249) as its source -- i.e. the verified/cleaned-question subset of the same underlying HLE benchmark, not a different eval. This mirrors an existing convention already in this corpus (see corpus/models/alibaba/qwen3-6-plus.json's hle entry, which explicitly treats a vendor's 'HLE-Verified' figure as equivalent to the base 'hle' slug when a concrete number is available). Seed2.1-Pro scores 42.9 in the no-tool configuration (GPT-5.5 50.4, Claude-4.7-Opus 46.9, Gemini-3.1-Pro 48.2, Seed2.1-Turbo 42.4); the model card separately reports a 'with Search' HLE-textonly figure (55.7) which is not used here to keep the no-tool convention consistent.

Labeled 'ArcAGI2' in Table 11 of the official model card (GPT-5.5 85.0, Claude-4.7-Opus 75.8, Gemini-3.1-Pro 77.1, Seed2.1-Turbo 61.3, Seed2.1-Pro 62.5). Flagging one citation inconsistency for transparency: the model card's own footnote reference for this row points to the ARC Prize's ARC-AGI-1 page (arcprize.org/arc-agi/1/) rather than an ARC-AGI-2-specific source, but the benchmark is explicitly and consistently named 'ArcAGI2' in both the table header and the surrounding prose, so it is mapped to the arc-agi-2 taxonomy slug on the strength of that explicit naming rather than the (likely just reused/stale) citation.

OSWorld, Table 4 ('Generalist Computer-Use Agent evaluation') of the official model card: Seed2.1-Pro reaches 78.8 under the 'Generalist CUA' setting (agent may issue Bash commands/scripts alongside GUI actions), up from a 72.6 GUI-only baseline -- this matches this corpus's existing 'osworld' slug convention (generic, non-'-verified' computer-use runs). GPT-5.5 78.7, Claude-4.7-Opus 82.8, Gemini-3.1-Pro 76.2, Seed2.1-Turbo 76.4. Flagging one internal inconsistency for transparency: Section 2.5.1's prose states 'Seed2.1-Pro reaches 78.2', which conflicts with Table 4 and Figure 5's narrative ('Success rises from 72.6% to 78.8% (Pro)') on the same result -- I used 78.8 since two independent parts of the document (table + figure caption) agree on it against the single conflicting prose sentence.

MathVista (no-tool), Table 9 ('Fundamental vision capability evaluation') of the official model card. Seed2.1-Pro scores 90.7, the best listed no-tool score (GPT-5.5 84.2, Gemini-3.1-Pro 90.2, Claude-4.7-Opus 84.4, Seed2.1-Turbo 90.5), confirmed in Section C.1's prose ('achieving the best no-tool performance on MathVista (90.7)...').

verificationNotes:
ByteDance's own blog and secondary coverage only make qualitative 'leads OSWorld/MobileWorld/MMMU-Pro/Terminal Bench 2.1/SWE-Pro/SciCode' claims without exact numbers, except for a non-standard 'Code Arena frontend' design-benchmark score of 1539 (#8, tied with Claude Opus 4.6) -- Code Arena/Design Arena is not one of the pre-approved benchmark slugs, so it is proposed here as a new slug rather than filed under an existing one.

| 2026-07-16: Checked all 8 missing-core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, gpqa, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu) across all three required source tiers. (1) Official materials: the seed.bytedance.com/en/seed2_1 launch page's full benchmark table (fetched directly) contains none of these eight -- it reports SuperGPQA instead of raw GPQA and MMMU-Pro instead of raw MMMU, i.e. deliberately harder successor evals, plus code/agent/vision-specific benchmarks (Terminal Bench 2.1, SWE-Atlas, NL2Repo-Bench, MathVision, CharXiv-RQ, etc.), never the legacy academic set. The Seed2_1_Model_Card.pdf (already cited elsewhere in this file) was unreachable via direct fetch this session (lf3-static.bytednsdoc.com and the lf6- mirror both fail DNS resolution from this environment), but howaiworks.ai/models/doubao-pro independently digested the same PDF -- its reproduced figures (GDPval 87.9, Agents' Last Exam 41.4, OSWorld 78.8 with the identical 78.2-vs-78.8 internal-inconsistency footnote already noted in this file) match this file's existing entries exactly, confirming it parsed the genuine document, and it explicitly states none of the 8 requested benchmarks appear in it. (2) Independent leaderboards: llm-stats.com/models/seed-2.1-pro lists mmlu/mmlu-pro/gpqa/math/aime-2025/humaneval/livecodebench/swe-bench-verified/mmmu as tracked categories but shows every one as score-pending for this model; Epoch AI's benchmarking hub (epoch.ai/benchmarks) does not carry Seed 2.1 Pro at all as of this date; Vals.ai's model list (vals.ai/models, checked 7/16/2026) likewise does not list it; llmreference.com states outright 'No headline benchmark score is tracked for Seed 2.1 Pro yet.' (3) Cross-reference: SuperCLUE's most recent published Chinese-leaderboard evaluation only covers Seed 2.0 Pro, not 2.1; no other model file in this corpus cites a Seed 2.1 Pro score for any of these eight slugs (only anthropic/claude-opus-4-8.json references Seed 2.1 Pro, and only for agents-last-exam, already in this file). Verdict: consistent, convergent absence across every checked source, not a search failure -- all 8 are recorded as omitted_no_evidence. This fits the corpus's known pattern of frontier labs skipping saturated legacy benchmarks (MMLU/GPQA/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU) in favor of harder successor evals (SuperGPQA, MMMU-Pro, Beyond AIME, Terminal-Bench 2.1/SWE-Atlas) for a 2026-era flagship launch; note 'Beyond AIME' (cited at 87.0 by secondary sources aitier.net/felloai.com) is a distinct, harder, non-equivalent benchmark from raw AIME and was deliberately NOT relabeled onto the 'aime' slug since it is not the same underlying evaluation and no 'beyond-aime' slug exists in this corpus's catalog.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep dive, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Re-chased the same four gaps using six source types deliberately NOT covered by the 2026-07-16 pass (which had exhausted official launch page, Model Card PDF, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com). All six were genuinely checked; none produced a citable number.

(1) Stanford HELM: fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest and /helm/classic/latest directly -- both are JS-rendered SPAs that returned no leaderboard content to the fetcher, so I corroborated via targeted search instead (`site:crfm.stanford.edu Seed 2.1`, "HELM leaderboard ByteDance Seed model added 2026"): zero indexed HELM pages reference Seed 2.1, Seed 2.0, Doubao, or ByteDance at all. HELM does not appear to evaluate this (closed, non-Western-API-distributed) model family at any version, not just 2.1.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: found and directly fetched ByteDance's own "Seed2.0 Model Card" arXiv paper (2607.00248, filed July 2026, i.e. after Seed 2.1's June 24 2026 launch) -- confirmed it does not mention "Seed 2.1" anywhere and reports no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers (it uses the same harder-successor-eval philosophy as the 2.1 launch materials). Also checked the sibling Seed1.8 Model Card arXiv paper (2603.20633). Ran four separate arXiv/Semantic-Scholar-targeted searches ("Seed 2.1 Pro" MMLU site:arxiv.org; Seed2.1 MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval baseline; Semantic Scholar citations) -- no paper using Seed 2.1 Pro as a baseline with any of these four scores turned up.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: searched for lm-evaluation-harness results.json / benchmark-repo run logs referencing "seed-2.1-pro" or "seed2.1-pro" -- none found. ByteDance-Seed's GitHub org does not appear to publish harness-run artifacts for the closed Seed 2.1 API models.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators outside the first pass's list: Artificial Analysis's MMLU-Pro leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/mmlu-pro) was fetched directly -- top models are Gemini 3 Pro Preview and Claude Opus 4.5; no ByteDance/Doubao/Seed model of any version is listed. Scale AI's SEAL/Labs leaderboards -- searched explicitly; no Seed 2.1 or Doubao entries found (one secondary source, tokenmix-style coverage, explicitly notes Seed 2.1 is absent from Scale's SWE-bench Pro leaderboard too). LiveBench.ai -- searched explicitly, zero hits tying it to Seed 2.1/Doubao/ByteDance. OpenRouter -- searched its bytedance-seed model-family page; it lists Seed-2.0-Lite/Mini but no Seed 2.1 Pro listing at all (likely not yet routed through OpenRouter). PapersWithCode -- searched for a Seed 2.1 Pro MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval leaderboard entry; none found.

(5) Non-English sources: fetched a Chinese "302.AI 基准实验室" (302.AI Benchmark Lab) independent third-party test writeup of Doubao Seed 2.1 Pro (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2053887114534817895) -- this is an independent Chinese evaluator that ran its own tests via API access, but its reported benchmarks are GDPVal, Agents' Last Exam, Terminal Bench 2.1, CharXiv-RQ, MeasureBench, TVBench and TOMATO -- i.e. the identical "harder successor eval" set as ByteDance's own materials, with no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers reported. Also attempted Baidu Baike's Doubao-Seed-2.1-Pro encyclopedia entry (baike.baidu.com/item/Doubao-Seed-2.1%20Pro/68025301) and wcode.net's model page, both of which failed with DNS resolution errors from this environment (same class of failure as the lf3-static.bytednsdoc.com block noted in the prior round), so they could not be checked directly; no other Chinese/Korean-language source surfaced by search reported these four benchmarks either.

(6) Sibling models: the ByteDance Seed2.0 Model Card (arXiv 2607.00248) and howaiworks.ai's Seed 2.0 Pro digest report Seed 2.0 Pro (the prior-generation sibling, not 2.1) at AIME2025=98.3 -- but this is a different model version and cannot be attributed to Seed 2.1 Pro under this corpus's per-model-version convention. Checked llm-stats.com's own Seed-2.0-Pro-vs-Seed-2.1-Pro side-by-side comparison page directly: for Seed 2.1 Pro it lists only CharXiv-D, MathVision, MathVista, MathVerse, Video-MME and BrowseComp -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval column for either model. No other sibling-model table (Seed2.1 Turbo reviews, launch coverage) was found citing Seed 2.1 Pro's score on these four benchmarks as a comparison point.

Verdict: convergent absence confirmed across all six new source-type categories (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub, alternate leaderboards, non-English community/independent-tester sources, and sibling-model tables). This reinforces round-1's finding that ByteDance and every independent evaluator that has touched Seed 2.1 Pro so far have uniformly skipped MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval in favor of harder 2026-era successor evals (SuperGPQA, MMMU-Pro, HLE, ArcAGI2, GDPval, Agents' Last Exam, etc.). All four gaps remain omitted_no_evidence; no new results added this round.

2026-07-18 (round-3 final pass, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Re-chased the same four gaps using five NEW source types not covered by round-1 (official materials/Epoch/Vals/LMArena/BenchLM/llm-stats.com) or round-2 (HELM/arXiv/GitHub-harness-logs/alternate leaderboards/non-English community/sibling-model tables). All five were genuinely attempted; none produced a citable number.

(1) Wayback Machine: attempted 8 separate direct-fetch requests (both via the WebFetch tool, which categorically refuses web.archive.org URLs, and via curl in Bash, spread across ~10 minutes with backoff) against archived snapshots of https://seed.bytedance.com/en/seed2_1 (targeting the 2026-06-23/24 launch window) and of llm-stats.com's Seed 2.1 Pro page. Every attempt returned HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) from web.archive.org itself -- the service is rate-limiting this environment outright (confirmed by hitting the bare web.archive.org root, which also 429'd), almost certainly residual load from this same multi-week research campaign having hit the Wayback Machine heavily across many other model files. One `archive.org/wayback/available` lookup (a lighter-weight endpoint, not rate-limited) did succeed and returned the closest snapshot for the launch page as 2026-06-23T04:57:12Z (http://web.archive.org/web/20260623045712/https://seed.bytedance.com/en/seed2_1) and for llm-stats.com's model page as 2026-06-27T00:19:45Z, but the actual snapshot content could not be retrieved through either tool path to compare against the live page. This source is honestly reported as attempted-but-inaccessible in this session, not as a confirmed clean check -- unlike the other four sources below, which were fully executed.

(2) Conference talks/keynotes: confirmed Seed 2.1 was launched at ByteDance's Volcano Engine FORCE 2026 conference (June 23-24, 2026) alongside Seedance 2.5 and Seedream 5.0. Searched extensively for a keynote video, transcript, or recap (YouTube, official Volcano Engine channels, Chinese tech press live-coverage of FORCE) -- found only qualitative claims ("rivals GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, and Gemini 3.1 Pro") in secondary coverage (xix.ai/live/5373, digg.com, kie.ai), never a benchmark slide or spoken figure for MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. No podcast appearance by Seed-team researchers discussing these specific benchmarks was found.

(3) Investor/press materials: ByteDance is a private company with no public earnings calls or SEC filings, so this source type was adapted to the closest available analog -- third-party equity/investor research on the private company. Fetched Sacra's "EQUITY RESEARCH ByteDance" report (sacra-pdfs.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/bytedance.pdf, updated 2026-06-15) in full; it covers Doubao MAU/revenue/monetization economics but contains zero benchmark figures of any kind for Seed 2.1 Pro (or any Seed model), let alone MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. No other investor-facing ByteDance disclosure (Sacra's ByteDance hub page, hellochinatech.com's monetization piece) cited these benchmarks either.

(4) GitHub issues/SDK repo discussions: confirmed via the GitHub API that the ByteDance-Seed org has no "Seed2.1" repository at all (only Seed2.0, Seed-1.8, Seed-Thinking-v1.5, seed-oss, etc. -- consistent with Seed 2.1 being fully closed/API-only with no OSS artifact). Ran targeted GitHub code/issue searches ("Seed 2.1" + MMLU, "Seed 2.1" + benchmark in:body, "doubao-seed-2.1") across all public GitHub issues/PRs -- the ~47-67 hits were exclusively third-party SDK/router/pricing-tracker PRs (lobehub, dify-official-plugins, models.dev, fastgpt-plugin, midscene, etc.) adding Doubao-Seed-2.1 as a routable model ID, one open still-unanswered LiveBench feature request (LiveBench/LiveBench#447, "Add Doubao Seed 2.1 Pro," zero comments), and one independent benchmark-tracking repo (echosoar/llm-score PR #5) whose own PR description explicitly lists Seed 2.1 Pro among the models that report only MMMU-Pro (not plain MMMU) -- corroborating, not contradicting, the existing convergent-absence finding. No engineer or maintainer reply anywhere supplied a real MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for this model.

(5) Third-party review/analysis sites: searched interconnects.ai (Nathan Lambert) directly -- it has covered ByteDance/Seed as an org (open-model rankings, Doubao MaaS revenue) but has no post specifically about Seed 2.1 Pro at all, let alone benchmark numbers. Searched ChinaTalk -- same result, its ByteDance/Doubao coverage stops at Seed 2.0 (Feb 2026), no Seed 2.1 piece found. Searched SemiAnalysis -- no hits referencing Seed 2.1. Checked felloai.com's "Seed 2.1 Pro Review: Matching Claude Opus 4.6" in full -- its only quantified benchmark is Design/Code Arena Frontend (1539, #8), no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Checked Chinese first-day launch coverage not yet read in prior rounds (36kr.com/p/3865585237660676, plus qbitai.com which failed with a DNS resolution error from this environment) -- 36kr's piece only repeats qualitative Terminal-Bench-2.1/SciCode/MCP-Atlas/Agents'-Last-Exam comparisons versus Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5, no numeric legacy-benchmark scores.

Verdict: four of the five newly-assigned source types were fully executed and converged on the same absence already established in rounds 1-2; the fifth (Wayback Machine) was genuinely attempted across 8 requests but was environmentally inaccessible (persistent 429 rate-limiting on web.archive.org) rather than checked-and-clean. Given this is the third independent research pass, spanning 15+ distinct source categories across all three rounds with uniform convergent absence, all four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) are recorded as omitted_no_evidence. This is consistent with the corpus's established pattern of 2026-era frontier labs (ByteDance Seed included) retiring the legacy MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval suite in favor of harder successor evals for flagship launches.
